stem: In triangles JKL and MNO, angles J and M each have measure 50°, JL = 12, and MO = 36. Which additional piece of information is sufficient to prove that triangle JKL is similar to triangle MNO?

answerOptions.content: The measures of angles K and O are 80° and 50°, respectively.
The measures of angles L and O are equal.

rationale: Triangles are similar if they have two pairs of congruent corresponding angles. Angles J and M (each 50°) are one such pair. If angle K = 80° and angle O = 50°, then in triangle JKL the third angle L = 180 − 50 − 80 = 50°, and in triangle MNO the third angle N = 180 − 50 − 50 = 80°. So angles K and N (corresponding) both equal 80°, confirming a second congruent pair and AA similarity.
